revision.text:
[[ja:伊久比売神社]] {{Expand Japanese|伊久比売神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ikuhime Shrine | native_name = 伊久比売神社 | image = Ikuhime jinjya.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ikuhime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.256389|135.171556}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Ikuhime Shrine'''|伊久比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kii Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11604948|ltq=Q11604948|qid=Q11604948|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kii Province}} ** Series ordinal: 13 ** P155: {{ill|Awashima Shrine|lt=Awashima Shrine|fr|Awashima-jinja|ja|淡嶋神社|ko|아와시마 신사|simple|Awashima Shrine|zh|淡嶋神社|qid=Q7007362|1=Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)}} ** P156: {{ill|Q17191190|ltq=Q17191190|qid=Q17191190|1=Asakura Shrine (Wakayama)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Wakayama (city)|lt=Wakayama|ar|واكاياما (واكاياما)|arz|واكاياما|azb|واکایاما (شهر)|az|Vakayama|ban|Wakayama, Wakayama|be|Вакаяма (горад)|bg|Вакаяма (град)|ca|Wakayama|ceb|Wakayama Shi|ce|Вакаяма (гӀала)|cs|Wakajama|cy|Wakayama|de|Wakayama|en|Wakayama (city)|envoyage|Wakayama|eo|Vakajamo|es|Wakayama|et|Wakayama|eu|Wakayama|fa|واکایاما (сахар)|pl|Wakayama|plvoyage|Wakayama|pt|Wakayama (cidade)|ro|Wakayama|ru|Вакаяма (город)|sc|Wakayama|sh|Wakayama|simple|Wakayama (city)|sr|Вакајама|sv|Wakayama|sw|Wakayama, Wakayama|szl|Wakayama|tg|Вакаяма|th|วากายามะ (เมือง)|tl|Lungsod ng Wakayama|tr|Wakayama|tt|Вакаяма (шәһәр)|tum|Wakayama|uk|Вакаяма|ur|واکایاما، واکایاما|uz|Vakayama|vi|Wakayama (thành phố)|war|Wakayama (syudad)|wuu|和歌山市|zh_classical|和歌山市|zh_min_nan|Wakayama-chhī|zh_yue|和歌山市|zh|和歌山市|qid=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}} * {{ill|Kii Province|lt=Kii Province|cs|Provincie Kii|de|Provinz Kii|en|Kii Province|es|Provincia de Kii|eu|Kii probintzia|fa|ولایت کیئی|fr|Province de Kii|gl|Provincia de Kii|id|Provinsi Kii|it|Kii (provincia)|ja|紀伊国|ko|기이국|lt|Kijo žemė|nl|Kii (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Kii|pt|Província de Kii|ru|Кии (провинция)|simple|Kii Province|sk|Kii (provincia)|sr|Провинција Киј|th|แคว้นคิอิ|tr|Kii (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Кії|vi|Tỉnh Kii|zh_min_nan|Kii-kok|zh|紀伊國|qid=Q864053|1=Kii Province}} * {{ill|Q11415547|ltq=Q11415547|qid=Q11415547|1=Nagusa district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.256389|135.171556}} == image (P18) == * Ikuhime jinjya.JPG == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183146 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917284|ltq=Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * Dineʼé ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いくひめじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9170005000284 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Ikuhimeno Shrine|lt=Ikuhimeno Shrine|qid=Q135041277|1=Ikuhimeno Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '和歌山県和歌山市市小路字木ノ下330', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:伊久比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11378800}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ikuhime Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Ikuhime_jinjya.JPG|270px]]
Approach |所在地 = Wakayama Prefecture, Wakayama City, Ichishōji Aza Kinoshita 330 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|15|23.0|N|135|10|17.6|E|region:JP-30_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ikuhime Shrine}} |祭神 = Ikuhime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=candidate Shikinaisha|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper-plate roof |別名 = Ichihime Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = October 13 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Ikuhime
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ikuhime Shrine''' (いくひめじんじゃ) is a shrine in Ichishōji, {{ill|lt=Wakayama|Q200747|qid=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|qid=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=candidate Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} identified with "Ikuhime Shrine" in Nagusa District, {{ill|lt=Kii Province|Q864053|qid=Q864053|1=Kii Province}}, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. Today it is revered as {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|qid=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} of Jirōmaru and Nobutoki in Kishi district, and of Kusumi and Nozaki districts in Wakayama City'Wakayamashi Yō' (revised 5th ed.), Wakayama City, 1965.. Former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#various shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deity === *'''Ikuhime-no-Mikoto''' The '{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|qid=Q11604964|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki}}' infers from the shrine location name "Ichishōji" and local calling of the deity as "Ichihime Daimyōjin" that a {{ill|lt=market|Q330284|qid=Q330284|1=Marketplace}} existed here before early modern times and "Ichihime" was enshrined as its guardian. The current deity is taken as Ikuhime-no-Mikoto, but there are other theories{{ill|lt=Yoshida Tōgo|Q11413910|qid=Q11413910|1=Tōgo Yoshida}}'s '{{ill|lt=Dai Nihon Chimei Jisho|Q17213149|qid=Q17213149|1=Dai Nippon Chimei Jisho}}' argues "I'''ku'''hime" may be an error for "I'''ya'''hime," paired with Iyato deity of {{ill|lt=Iyatome Shrine|Q11458562|qid=Q11458562|1=Iyato Shrine}} in Muatani, that Iyato deity is Ōyabiko-no-Kami (another name of {{ill|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Kami|Q10881556|qid=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}), and thus Iya-hime may be {{ill|lt=Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyatsuhime}}. Also, {{ill|lt=Deguchi Nobutsune|Q108780926|qid=Q108780926|1=Deguchi Nobutsune}}'s '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|qid=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}' (completed 1733) and '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nanki Jinja-roku|1=UNKNOWN}}' (published 1746) identify the deity as {{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|qid=Q11596349|1=Wakahiru-me}}.. === Origin === Founding origin is unknown, but a tradition says that in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|qid=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8 (764), when foreign enemies attacked, a person named Fujiwara Sadakuni was appointed general to subdue them, and after victorious return he received divine revelation and offered sacred gifts'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kii Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'.. If so, the shrine existed before the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|qid=Q189178|1=Nara period}}. By shrine tradition, it is the "Ikuhime Shrine" of the Engishiki list and the "Senior Fourth Upper Ikuhime deity" among 30 earthly deities in Nagusa District of the '{{ill|lt=Kii no Kuni Jinmyōchō|Q17210008|qid=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}'. However, '{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|qid=Q11604964|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki}}' states that because this lower {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinokawa|1=UNKNOWN}} area was repeatedly flooded and later saw warfare, origins are unclear. From its old name "Ichihime Daimyōjin" and local place name Ichishōji, it reasons that a market opened in this area before the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, and this shrine was invited as its {{ill|lt=guardian deity|Q131278957|qid=Q131278957|1=Guardian deities}} after villages formed as the coastline retreated. After {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yorinobu|Q1045763|qid=Q1045763|1=Tokugawa Yorinobu}} entered Kii, old traces were examined and this shrine was identified and renamed; then in {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|qid=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 11 (1726), precinct boundaries were set and it came to appear as an ancient shrine, becoming ubusunagami of four villages in Kusumi-shōNot a shōen estate but an early-modern settlement unit derived from the ancient/medieval Kusumi-gō. (Jirōmaru, Ichishōji, Naka-no, FunadokoCorresponding to present-day Oaza Jirōmaru, Ichishōji, Kusumi-naka, and Funadoko.)'Kii Zoku Fudoki.' According to 'Kusumi-shō Ikuhime Shrine Saikenza-chō' (in 'Wakayamashi-shi' vol.6), an order recognizing it as Shikinaisha was issued June 28, Kyōhō 11, and by Feb 3 of the next year a notice board was erected stating Shikinaisha status and "no killing" in precinct.. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}) April it was ranked village shrine; from Meiji 41 to Meiji 45 (Feb), nearby shrines were merged or relocated into its precinct. === Engishiki "Ikuhime Shrine" === The Engishiki shrine's whereabouts were already unknown before early modern times. As above, this shrine came to be identified after Yorinobu entered Kii, but '{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|qid=Q11604964|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki}}' was skeptical, saying this land was under sea before Engishiki and "the site shape is not that of a great shrine," suggesting that even if this were the Engishiki shrine, it may have been moved from a site to the northeast. In contrast, physical geography confirms Kinokawa channel changes but shows this site was not under sea or river channel in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, so a local ubusunagami-type shrine may have long stood at current location'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten.''Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture.'. As this area was an inlet coast in antiquity and later had Kii port with an adjacent market through medieval times, a shrine to "Ichihime" as market guardian may have been founded. Even in that case, it does not necessarily mean that shrine was Engishiki-listed, and since identification occurred only from Edo onward, substantial room for re-examination remains. === Ritual organization === In early modern times, a {{ill|lt=miyaza|Q11454763|qid=Q11454763|1=miyaza}} of four villages (Jirōmaru, Ichishōji, Naka-no, Funadoko) is said to have held hereditary rights to participate in shrine rites'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku.' In the above Saikenza-chō, 17 members are known: 3 from Naka-no, 7 from Ichishōji, 4 from Funadoko, 3 from Jirōmaru., though details are now unclear'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku.'. Soon after domain recognition as Shikinaisha, in June Kyōhō 13, Tanaka Tōbei of Ichishōji village in the miyaza was appointed {{ill|lt=kannushi|Q15888735|qid=Q15888735|1=Kannushi (disambiguation)}} as "person of proper lineage" by the domainAbove Saikenza-chō.. Current annual festival day is October 13. === Shrine buildings === The honden is {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|lt=ikken-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=copper-plate roof|1=UNKNOWN}} and has {{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|qid=Q87220234|1=chigi and katsuogi}}. The haiden is a tiled {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=wari-haiden|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Massha === Sato-gami ShrineFormer unranked shrine in Funadoko., Hachiman-sha and Hachioji-sha (one building, {{ill|lt=aiden|Q135009883|qid=Q135009883|1=Aidono}})Former unranked shrine in Ichishōji Aza Gongen, relocated in Meiji 43., Sumiyoshi ShrineFormer unranked shrine in Kusumi-naka Aza Danzuka, relocated in Meiji 41., Konpira-gūFormer unranked shrine in Hirai., Kusumoto Hakuryū Ōkami, Myōken-sha, Hachiman-sha, Hachiman Shrine, Kuzu ShrineFormer unranked shrine in Awa., Kasuga Shrine, and a small stone pagoda carved with "Ujisha Ryō Daimyōjin / Amaterasu Kōtai Jingū, Konpira Daigongen." Most were relocated at the end of Meiji as noted above. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Kishu domain ed., 'Kii Zoku Fudoki' (reprint by Wakayama Prefecture shrine supervision office), Teikoku Chihō Gyōsei Gakkai Publishing, 1910 * Sugihara Yasushige, 'Nanki Jinja-roku' (in 'Shintō Taikei' Jinja-hen vol.41 Kii/Awaji), Shintō Taikei Hensan-kai, 1987 * Takaichi Shiyū, 'Kii Meisho Zue' 1 (retitled reprint by Rekishi Tosho-sha), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Rekishi Tosho-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1970 * {{ill|lt=Yoshida Tōgo|Q11413910|qid=Q11413910|1=Tōgo Yoshida}}, 'Dai Nihon Chimei Jisho' vol.2 (Kamigata), {{ill|lt=Fuzanbō|Q11395102|qid=Q11395102|1=Fuzambo}}, 1980 (expanded reprint of 1900 edition) * 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku' vol.23 Nankaidō, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University Press|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1987 * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten' vol.30 (Wakayama), Kadokawa Shoten, 1985 ISBN 4-04-001300-X * 'Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture' (Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 31), Heibonsha, 1983 ISBN 9784582490312 * 'Wakayama-ken-shi' vol.3, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Shuppan|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1970 (reprint of Wakayama Prefecture edition, 1914) * 'Wakayama-ken Jinja-shi,' Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1995 * 'Wakayamashi-shi' vol.6 (Early modern sources II), Wakayama City, 1976 === Related item === * {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Shrine (Wakayama)|Q11467400|qid=Q11467400|1=Yamaguchi Shrine}} - shrine that 'Kii Zoku Fudoki' suspected might be the original Engishiki "Ikuhime Shrine" === External links === * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1002 Ikuhime Shrine] (Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ikuhime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ikuhime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]]
